ranked_scenarios.summary: Password not hidden after clicking 'Hide password' button while creating new login on 'about:logins' page.

description.text:

Screen Recording 2023-05-04 at 15.21.04.mov

[Affected versions]:

114.0a1 (2023-05-03) (64-bit)

[Affected Platforms]:

macOS 13.3.1 (22E261)

[Steps to reproduce]:

1. Open the latest Nightly browser.
2. Navigate to "about:logins" page.
3. Click the "Create new login" button.
4. Enter the password.
5. Click the "Show password" button. 
6. Click the "Hide password" button.

[Expected Results]:
The password should be hidden.

[Actual Results]:
The password is still being displayed.

ranked_scenarios.summary: Issues with "Create New Login" form

description.text:
Created attachment 9077836
image.png

- "Save Changes" should use a different string, since we're creating a new login
- That same button should be blue, not gray
- The save button should have `type="submit"` and the "Cancel" button should have `type="button"`
- The click event handler for the save button should move to be a submit event handler on the parent form

ranked_scenarios.summary: Remove caching of login object on the login-list-items

description.text: With bug 1566115 we now have a map of guid -> {login, listItem} and as such we don't need to cache the login object on the listItem.

ranked_scenarios.summary: Password field should be unmasked when editing

description.text:
## Steps to reproduce

1. Open browser and navigate to "about:logins" page
2. Activate the "Create New Login" button
3. Navigate to the "Password" field
4. Fill in the password field with any characters

## Expected result

* Password is unmasked

## Actual result

* Password is masked

katieC: Does this sound like we discussed earlier on 7/22/20? We discussed a few different bugs, I just want to make sure this particular bug is accurate.

ranked_scenarios.summary: Link users to the login they are duplicating in the error message

description.text: When a user creates a login, we should check that the hostname, username, and password are not already present in the storage (with a wildcard formSubmitURL).

ranked_scenarios.summary: Master password prompt before copying/revealing passwords in about:logins

description.text: To prevent snooping and parity with the old management UI, we need to prompt for MP before copying/revealing passwords.

ranked_scenarios.summary: Add telemetry probes to measure breach alerts and interactions

description.text:
To measure the effects of new breach alert indicators, we need to add telemetry events:

* How many breach alert indicators are shown in the login list?
* How many times is a breached login viewed?
* (Optional) How many times is the "Learn more about this breach" link clicked? (Optional because we can kinda-sorta get this number from Google Analytics on the website)
* How many times is the website of a breached login visited? (Fuzzy indicator that the user intends to change their password?)

If/when we have all these probes, we should be able to measure how many people are following the intended "conversion" path. I.e.,

See breach alert -> view breached login -> learn more about breach -> visit website (ostensibly to change their password)

We might even be able to see if the number of breach alert indicators shown in the login list goes DOWN after users click the learn more link and/or visit the website.

ranked_scenarios.summary: Address feedback from UX review #1 of about:logins

description.text:
- On initial page load, if a login exists default to selecting the first login
- No border on the copy button when clicked
- Website address should be clickable and look like a link
- Focus the search box on page load

Completed in other bugs:
- Lockwise logo should use text fill color (bug 1559303)
- Use monospace font for password (bug 1553209)
- Website address should be editable (will be fixed by bug 1560359)

ranked_scenarios.summary: Code cleanup in about:logins

description.text:
- Add in a <form> and use form.reset() to reset form validation
- Pull out a reference to classList in handleEvent functions
- Add jsdoc to methods of custom elements
- Move custom attributes to element.dataset
- Make method naming style consistent (_prefix vs no _prefix)
- Order methods consistently

ranked_scenarios.summary: Re-mask passwords in about:logins after primary password timeout

description.text:
User Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64; rv:83.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/83.0

Steps to reproduce:

Opened the Password Manager with the primary Password.


Actual results:

The manager opened, but never closed again.


Expected results:

It would be nice it it would close from time to time, and or close when the lid closes/the screen is turned off (for mobile versions) or be able to close it via a signal or CLI call.

ranked_scenarios.summary: AutoScroll shouldn't start when middle clicking on inputs in aboutlogins

description.text:
AutoScroll shouldn't be started when middle clicking on about:logins input[name=origin], see [1], because the input handles middle click itself and opens URL associated with the input. It's weird to see autoscroll icon on the screen and then seeing a new tab open.

Input catches click event [2] unlike AutoScroll which handles mousedown, thus input can't prevent default action which would prevented autoscroll as well. So the suggestion is to introduce 'noautoscroll' attribute and make AutoScroll to ignore such elements.

Marking this bug blocking bug 1597765, because current behavior breaks test_login_item.html test.

https://searchfox.org/mozilla-central/source/browser/components/aboutlogins/content/aboutLogins.html#185
[2] https://searchfox.org/mozilla-central/source/browser/components/aboutlogins/content/components/login-item.js#397

description.text:

[Affected Versions]:

[Affected Platforms]

[Steps to reproduce]:
1. Open the latest Nightly browser with the profile from prerequisites.
2. Navigate to "about:logins" page.
3. Click one of the saved logins.
4. Click the "Copy" button.
5. Select another login from the list and observe the "Copy" button.
 
[Expected results]:

[Actual results]:
- The "Copied!" string from the previous login persist on the selected one.

[Notes]:
- Attached a screen recording with the issue.

ranked_scenarios.summary: Do a formatting pass on mozilla-central using Prettier (the actual change)

description.text:
The actual formatting pass for bug 1558517

It's worth pointing out that this series becomes bitrotten on upload. These commits are useful for
1. Dong a full formatting pass close to landing day and making sure we can address all known problems so far.
2. Pinging the appropriate module owners and making sure they see what's going on.

As it stands, this final series serves as a guidance for that's probably extremely close to what's landing on July 5. Rebasing is not an option for generating these commits, which will be re-done.

ranked_scenarios.summary: Don't include paths in the origin field of manually added logins

description.text: Bug 1550109 used `type=url` but didn't enforce that the URL was an origin without a path component. This is important to fix to not get invalid data in storage and Sync which will cause us pain for years to come in bug reports.

ranked_scenarios.summary: Missing Create New Login > Cancel button when there are no other logins

Created attachment 9245013
missing-cancel.png

It looks similar to https://bugzilla.mozilla.org/show_bug.cgi?id=1576735

ranked_scenarios.summary: The Website link from the Lockwise manage page can't be opened using middle click

description.text:
Created attachment 9069586
link middle click.gif

[Affected Versions]:
- Lockwise 2.2.3-alpha
- Nightly 68.0a1

[Affected Platforms]
- All Windows
- All Mac
- All Linux

[Perquisites]
- Have the Firefox 68 beta or latest Nightly 69 version installed.
- Have the Lockwise 2.2.3 - alpha extension installed from [Lockwise website](https://lockwise.firefox.com/).
- Have multiple saved logins.

[Steps to reproduce]:
1. Open the Firefox browser with the profile from prerequisites.
2. Click the "Lockwise" toolbar button and click the "Open Lockwise" button from the doorhanger.
3. Click one of the saved logins from the manage page.
4. Middle click on the website link.
5. Observe the behavior.

[Expected results]:
- The website is opened in a new tab.

[Actual results]:
- Nothing happens.

[Regression]:
- This issue is not reproducible on Firefox versions lower than 68. Considering this using mozregression tools I have managed to find the pushlog with the changes that caused this regression.
- Here is the pushlog: https://tinyurl.com/y6kndlly
- It seems that [bug 1379466](https://bugzilla.mozilla.org/show_bug.cgi?id=1379466) introduced this issue on Firefox 68.

[Notes]:
- The issue is not reproducible on Firefox 67 release.
- The issue is also reproducible if you try to open the website by pressing the "Ctrl" key + "Left Click". The website is not opened.
- The issue is also logged in Lockwsie Github repository in [#255](https://github.com/mozilla-lockwise/lockwise-addon/issues/255).
- Attached a screen recording with the issue.

ranked_scenarios.summary: Base page for HTML-based login manager

description.text:
This bug tracks implementing the base page for an HTML-based in-content login manager.

More functionality and styling will come in later bugs. This bug tracks the communication between LoginManager, the HTML page, and implements some basic functionality to verify that the communication is working.

ranked_scenarios.summary: Replace all non-user-facing references to "master" password

description.text: The term "master" in software makes reference to the "Master/Slave" relationship. As one action to uproot this problematic terminology (see Meta bug), we should replace it. Bug 1644807 dealt with user-facing references while this bug will deal with the remaining non-user-facing references in code and code comments.

ranked_scenarios.summary: Show a 'Discard' dialog when canceling or leaving the edit/create mode in about:logins

description.text:
Similar to the delete dialog, if the user enters Edit mode and makes any changes then tries to select a different login or click the Cancel button we should show a dialog asking them if they are sure they want to discard their changes.

Similar for the Create mode for new logins.

As we'd like to avoid an onunload event listener due to issues with the privileged content process (bug 1548997), I would prefer that we do not show the discard dialog when the page is unloaded.

ranked_scenarios.summary: The copy username button became active if the password of a login that doesn't contain a username is copied

description.text:
Created attachment 9196596
Copy username button.gif

**[Affected versions]:**
- Firefox Release 84.0.2
- Firefox Beta 85.0b7
- Nightly 86.0a1

**[Affected Platforms]:**
- Windows 10 x64
- Mac 10.15
- Linux Mint 20 

**[Prerequisites]:**
- Have a login saved that doesn't contain a username.

**[Steps to reproduce]:**
1. Open the browser with the profile from prerequisites.
2. Navigate to "about:logins" page.
3. Select the saved login that doesn't have a username.
4. Click the "Copy" password button.
5. Observe the "Copy" username button.

**[Expected result]:**
 - The button remains inactive.
 
**[Actual result]:**
- The button became active.

**[Notes]:**
- I don't think that this is a regression since I can reproduce this on Firefox 72 release.
- Attached a screen recording of the issue.

ranked_scenarios.summary: Protect login export behind Master Password and/or OS Reauthentication

description.text:
If the user has a master password set or OS re-authentication is enabled we should prompt for them before exporting.

For the OS re-auth prompt we would need two strings: one for Windows and on for macOS [like the ones for reveal/copy/edit](https://searchfox.org/mozilla-central/rev/bc3600def806859c31b2c7ac06e3d69271052a89/browser/locales/en-US/browser/aboutLogins.ftl#107-127).

ranked_scenarios.summary: Add telemetry probe for clicks on the vulnerable password learn more notification

description.text: For https://searchfox.org/mozilla-central/rev/a707541ff423ade0d81cef6488e6ecfa09273886/browser/components/aboutlogins/content/components/login-item.js#502

ranked_scenarios.summary: Copy button timers are misleading

description.text:
Selecting “Copy” for a given item should reset the timer for all “Copy” actions.

If you hit copy on both username and password in quick succession, they both maintain their “Copied” status, when in reality, we should revert back to the initial state of a button if another button is pressed -- since only one of those copied items is actually still on the clipboard.

ranked_scenarios.summary: Review LoginItem localization with Fluent before exposing it to localization

description.text:
Right now, the `login-item` element has a huge number of attributes, which really shows a weakness of how we set up DOM element localization in the Fluent bindings.

Once LoginItem is feature-complete, we should review this and see if we can do better. In particular, a few child elements with a slot might help a long way.

Not only would they make the localization easier, but they'd also help in maintaining the element, in case some of its strings need changing or addition. Right now, the whole translation for the element needs to be redone.

ranked_scenarios.summary: The master password should not be requested more times in a row

description.text:
**Note**
* When the user attempts to edit a login, the master password is requested too many times in a row.

**Affected versions**
* Nightly v70.0a1

**Affected platforms**
* All

**Steps to reproduce**
1. Open browser.
2. Save any login.
3. Set a master password.
(Restart browser.)
4. Open the "about:logins" page.
Observe: Master password is requested. Type it in.
5. Click the "edit" button to edit a login.
6. Click the "eye-like" icon to reveal the login password.
Observe: Master password is requested. Type it in.
7. Change anything to the saved login (Website Address/Username/Password).
8. Click the "Save Changes" button.
Observe: Master password is requested. Type it in.

**Expected result**
* The master password is not requested so often.

**Actual result**
* The Master Password is requested too often.

ranked_scenarios.summary: The password of a saved login can be revealed with devtools without re-entering the master password if one is set

description.text:
Created attachment 9095453
password revealed without entering master password.gif

[Affected Versions]:
- Nightly 71.0a1
- Beta 70.0b9

[Affected Platforms]
- All Windows
- All Mac
- All Linux

[Prerequisites]
- Have multiple logins saved.
- Have a master password set.

[Steps to reproduce]:
1. Open the latest Nightly build with the profile from prerequisites.
2. Navigate to a login page and enter the MP to unlock it (eg: "https://www.facebook.com/").
3. Open about:logins and select any saved password from the login list.
4. Right-click on the password and select the "Inspect Element" option.
5. From the Inspector tool change the input's argument from "password" to "text" and press "Ente" key.
6. Observe the password from login-item.

[Expected results]:
- The password is not revealed.

[Actual results]:
- The password is revealed.

[Notes]:
- Attached a screen recording with the issue.

ranked_scenarios.summary: The “Discard Unsaved Changes?” pop-up is not displayed if the “Edit” mode of a saved login is opened while logging into the website using the credentials from the selected login

description.text:
Created attachment 9086984
updatesWronglyDisplayed.mp4

[Affected versions]:
- Firefox latest Nightly 70.0a1 (2019-08-20) (64-bit)

[Affected Platforms]:
- Mac 10.14
- Windows 10 x64
- Ubuntu 16.04 x64

[Prerequisites]:
- Have at least one saved login.

[Steps to reproduce]:
1. Open the latest Nightly browser with the profile from prerequisites.
2. Navigate to "about:logins" page and click one of the saved logins.
3. Click on the “Edit” button and edit the username and password.
4. Click on the website address and logging in.
5. Focus back to the “about:logins” page.
6. Observe the behavior.

[Expected result]:
- The “Discard Unsaved Changes?” pop-up is displayed.
- The password and the username are not updated.
Or
- The “Edit” mode is still opened.

[Actual result]:
- The “Edit” mode is dismissed.
- The changes from the Username field are displayed until the page is refreshed or another login is selected.
- The Password field seems to be unchanged, but if the “Edit” button is clicked the previously added characters to the password are displayed.

[Notes]:
- This behavior does not affect the future logins on the website because the changes are not saved. 
- Attached a screen recording with the issue.

ranked_scenarios.summary: Clicking “Create New Login” button while editing and having unsaved changes should prompt for confirmation

description.text:
STR:
- Create a login
- edit that login without saving
- Click "Create new login"

AR:
"Create new login" screen is shown without prompting for confirmation

ER:
Page should prompt for confirmation whether you want to discard the changes

ranked_scenarios.summary: Upgrade Prettier to the latest version

description.text:
Prettier hasn't been updated for a few years, and it is about time that we did so. There's [various improvements and new command line arguments](https://prettier.io/blog/) to Prettier that have happened since 1.9.x.

Mainly due to the changes from 1.9 -> 2.0, there are quite a few places where we'll need to reformat the tree. Hence I'm planning on landing this during the 115 nightly cycle in May, before the next ESR happens.

ranked_scenarios.summary: Logins that lack a username show a LoginListItem with only the hostname

description.text:
In about:logins, logins that lack usernames appear as just the hostname in the list of logins and the height of the item is shorter than the other items.

We should show some default text like "No username" in italics or just show a blank line where the username would have been.

ranked_scenarios.summary: Create CustomElement for toggling text on a input field

description.text: This custom element will show the masked password as a fixed number of characters regardless of password length, and upon revealing will replace the masked characters with a input[type=password] textbox. Masking the characters will revert the behavior. Password changes will still be committed if the password is revealed, changed, hidden, then saved.

ranked_scenarios.summary: Edit stored login details

description.text: The LoginItem should be editable and the user should be able to save edits to the login details. The lastUpdatedTime should be updated accordingly.

ranked_scenarios.summary: Button to open a site from a login item

description.text:
Users should have the ability to open a site for the login that they are viewing. The button should appear next to the hostname in the LoginItem view.

Implementation note: The login URL opened from the page should not be trusted. The site-open event should be passed to the parent process which can then retrieve the login hostname from storage and open the site in an adjacent tab.

ranked_scenarios.summary: Remove link to the breach details from about:logins

description.text: Upcoming changes to Monitor will break existing links.

ranked_scenarios.summary: Implement design spec for Website Breach Banner / Notification

description.text:
Created attachment 9105090
website-breach-24.svg

Invision spec: https://mozilla.invisionapp.com/share/BEU7ZBJ486Y

• Pref-off dismissal code/UI
• add link to origin
• show breach date
• string changes
• change colours 
• platform border-radius
• learn more link to SUMO

ranked_scenarios.summary: Password toggle button breaks after specifics steps in about:logins

description.text:
Created attachment 9099850
password_icon.png

**Affected versions**
* 71.0a1 (2019-10-08)
* 70.0b13

**Affected platforms**
* Ubuntu 16.04
* Windows 10
* macOS 10.14

**Steps to reproduce**
1. Access about:logins
2. Click on any saved login;
3. Edit this login, click on the "Edit" button;
4. Click on the show/hide toggle button;
5. Click on "Save Changes" button;

**Expected result**
* The password show/hide toggle button is cut off and the label is "Hide password".

**Actual result**
* The password show/hide toggle button is not cut off and the label is "Hide password".

**Regression range**
* not a regression, visible with Bug 1560431

**Additional notes**
* attached screenshot illustrating the issue;

ranked_scenarios.summary: The website address link is no longer opened if it's clicked

description.text:
Created attachment 9098757
link is not opened on click.gif

[Affected Versions]:
- Nightly 71.0a1

[Affected Platforms]
- All Windows
- All Mac
- All Linux

[Prerequisites]:
- Have at least one saved logins.

[Steps to reproduce]:
1. Open the latest Nightly browser and navigate to "about:logins" page.
2. Select any of the saved logins.
3. Right click on the website address link.
4. Observe the behavior.

[Expected results]:
- The website is opened in a new tab.

[Actual results]:
- Nothing happens.

[Regression]:
-  The issue is not reproducible with older Nightly 71.0a1 (2019-09-03) build. Considering this, using mozregression tool I have found a pushlog which contains the issue that broke this in the latest Nightly build. Here are the results:
 - Pushlog: https://tinyurl.com/y39ej5a9
 - Last good revision: ff09f16f5b3d5310a96675f53671b622a5aa1e5e
 - First bad revision: faaf976800c27b0f423a69deb5fd6a6dd557de98

This pushlog contains multiple patches and we are not sure which one introduced this behavior.
@Jared can you please take a look?

[Notes]:
- The issue is not reproducible if you are using the middle click. The website is correctly opened in a new tab if you are using the middle click.
- Attached a screen recording with the issue.
